title: Methods for Accessing I/O Devices
description: A flowchart illustrating the three primary methods a CPU uses to communicate with and control I/O devices: Programmed I/O (Polling), Interrupt-Driven I/O, and Direct Memory Access (DMA).
relationships: CPU polls Device Status Register (Programmed I/O)
I/O Device sends signal via IRQ (Interrupt-Driven I/O)
DMA Controller manages data transfer between I/O Device and Main Memory (DMA)
